subject: Geography
title: Scandinavia's Ancient Spine
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Scandinavia's Ancient Spine.

hook.text: The Scandinavian Mountains run the length of Norway and Sweden like a spine.

buildup.text: They're over 400 million years old—once as tall as the Himalayas, now worn gentle.

discovery.text: Norwegian fjords were carved by glaciers that once buried these mountains in ice.

twist.text: The land is still rising—rebounding from the weight of ice that melted 10,000 years ago.

climax.text: Scandinavia gains about one centimeter of height per decade from this glacial rebound.

punchline.text: The ice left. The land is still standing back up.

quiz.question: Why is Scandinavia still rising in elevation?
options: Glacial rebound from ice that melted 10,000 years ago
Volcanic activity pushing the land up
